summary: War between the Olympian gods and the Titans
notes: Major divine succession conflict in Greek myth

summary: The gods create Pandora as part of Zeus punishment for humanity
notes: Linked to Prometheus and Epimetheus

summary: Hades takes Persephone to the Underworld
notes: Explains seasonal cycle in later tradition

summary: Paris judges between Hera Athena and Aphrodite
notes: Important mythical cause of the Trojan War

summary: Helen leaves Sparta with Paris for Troy
notes: Traditions vary on agency and responsibility

summary: Greek leaders gather before sailing to Troy
notes: Linked to sacrifice of Iphigenia

summary: Iphigenia is offered before the Greek expedition sails
notes: Some traditions say Artemis rescues her

summary: Achilles withdraws from battle after dispute with Agamemnon
notes: Central conflict of the Iliad

summary: Hector kills Patroclus in battle
notes: This causes Achilles to return to combat

summary: Achilles kills Hector outside Troy
notes: One of the major climactic events of the Iliad

summary: Troy is captured by the Greeks
notes: Covered in later Trojan War traditions rather than fully in the Iliad

summary: Aeneas escapes Troy and begins his journey toward Italy
notes: Central Roman continuation of Trojan myth

summary: Odysseus blinds the Cyclops Polyphemus
notes: Leads to Poseidon hostility toward Odysseus

summary: Odysseus encounters Circe on Aiaia
notes: Circe transforms some of his crew

summary: Calypso keeps Odysseus on Ogygia
notes: One of the delays in his return home

summary: Odysseus returns home and confronts the suitors
notes: Major nostos or return narrative

summary: Clytemnestra kills Agamemnon after his return from Troy
notes: Central event in the Oresteia

summary: Orestes kills Clytemnestra to avenge Agamemnon
notes: Raises questions of justice family duty and pollution

summary: Orestes is tried and purified at Athens
notes: Mythic origin story for legal judgement

summary: Jason and the Argonauts travel to Colchis
notes: Major heroic quest cycle

summary: Medea helps Jason complete impossible tasks
notes: Shows Medea as helper sorceress and strategist

summary: Medea takes revenge after Jason abandons her
notes: Central event of Euripides Medea

summary: Theseus enters the Labyrinth and kills the Minotaur
notes: Ariadne helps him with the thread

summary: Daedalus and Icarus fly from Crete
notes: Icarus falls after flying too close to the sun

summary: Perseus beheads Medusa
notes: Pegasus is born from Medusa in later tradition

summary: Oedipus defeats the Sphinx by solving her riddle
notes: Leads to his kingship at Thebes

summary: Oedipus learns his true identity and crimes
notes: Central revelation in Oedipus Rex

summary: Antigone defies Creon and buries her brother
notes: Conflict between state law and family duty

summary: Dionysus punishes Thebes for denying his divinity
notes: Central event of The Bacchae

summary: Orpheus attempts to retrieve Eurydice from the dead
notes: One of the best-known katabasis myths
